title: Vagator Beach
tagline: Vagator Beach — beach town of Goa

overview.short: Vagator Beach a beach in Bardez Taluka, in the state of Goa, India.
overview.description: Vagator Beach a beach in Bardez Taluka, in the state of Goa, India. It is located across the Chapora River from the town of Morjim.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Chapora Fort
topPlaces.description: Chapora Fort (Konkani pronunciation: [ʃɑːpoːɾɑː], Devanagari: शापोरा), located in Bardez, Goa, rises high above the Chapora River. The site is the location of a fort built by Adil Shahi dynasty ruler Adil Shah and called Shahpoora, whose name was altered to Shapora (chapora) by Malvankar on the request of the Portuguese.

topPlaces.name: Anjuna
topPlaces.description: Anjuna (, an-JOO-nə; Konkani pronunciation: [ɦɔɳzuɳẽ]) is a census town located on the coast of North Goa, India. It is one of the twelve comunidades of Bardez.

topPlaces.name: St. Michael's Church, Anjuna
topPlaces.description: St. Michael's Church is a historic Roman Catholic church located in Anjuna, Goa state, India.

topPlaces.name: Siolim
topPlaces.description: Siolim ([ʃiwoːlĩː]) is a village in Bardez taluka, and a census town on the central west coast of India, in the North Goa district of Goa. The 2001 population was 10,311, and 10,936 in 2011.

topPlaces.name: Zagor (folk drama)
topPlaces.description: Zagor (Konkani: जागर), (nocturnal vigil), mainly celebrated in Siolim, in Bardez taluka of Goa is a festival including dance, drama and music.

topPlaces.name: 2025 Arpora nightclub fire
topPlaces.description: On 6 December 2025, a fire killed 25 people and injured 50 at the Birch by Romeo Lane nightclub in Arpora, Goa, India. The incident occurred when the club was hosting a dance party.

topPlaces.name: Ponolem
topPlaces.description: Ponolem (or Punolem) is a village in North Goa's Bardez taluka (sub-district), India. It lies in the Goa Assembly constituency of Aldona.

topPlaces.name: Assagao
topPlaces.description: Assagao is a village in Bardez, Goa, India located about 4 kilometres west of Mapusa. It is known as the "Land of Flowers" as well as several other nicknames – "Goa’s South Extension", "Artist’s village", "The Beverly Hills of Goa", "Goa’s Tuscany".

items.note: Chapora Fort (Konkani pronunciation: [ʃɑːpoːɾɑː], Devanagari: शापोरा), located in Bardez, Goa, rises high above the Chapora River. The site is the location of a fort built by Adil Shahi dynasty ruler Adil Shah and called Shahpoora, whose name was altered to Shapora (chapora) by Malvankar on the request of the Portuguese.
Anjuna (, an-JOO-nə; Konkani pronunciation: [ɦɔɳzuɳẽ]) is a census town located on the coast of North Goa, India. It is one of the twelve comunidades of Bardez.
St. Michael's Church is a historic Roman Catholic church located in Anjuna, Goa state, India.
Siolim ([ʃiwoːlĩː]) is a village in Bardez taluka, and a census town on the central west coast of India, in the North Goa district of Goa. The 2001 population was 10,311, and 10,936 in 2011.
Zagor (Konkani: जागर), (nocturnal vigil), mainly celebrated in Siolim, in Bardez taluka of Goa is a festival including dance, drama and music.
On 6 December 2025, a fire killed 25 people and injured 50 at the Birch by Romeo Lane nightclub in Arpora, Goa, India. The incident occurred when the club was hosting a dance party.
Ponolem (or Punolem) is a village in North Goa's Bardez taluka (sub-district), India. It lies in the Goa Assembly constituency of Aldona.

Assagao is a village in Bardez, Goa, India located about 4 kilometres west of Mapusa. It is known as the "Land of Flowers" as well as several other nicknames – "Goa’s South Extension", "Artist’s village", "The Beverly Hills of Goa", "Goa’s Tuscany".

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Vagator Beach?
faq.a: The best time to visit Vagator Beach is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Vagator Beach?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Manohar International Airport, Mopa (North Goa) (20 km) and the nearest railway station is Vasco da Gama (Goa) (23 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Vagator Beach?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Vagator Beach at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Vagator Beach?
faq.a: Stays in Vagator Beach start from ₹1,200 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Vagator Beach?
faq.a: The top attractions are Chapora Fort, Anjuna, St. Michael's Church, Anjuna, Siolim, Zagor (folk drama).
